creator: avatar@brahms.mmlab.cse.yzu.edu.tw
text:
Attached is the patch which enables basic ESS based soundcard
support for VoxWare sound driver(/sys/i386/isa/sound).

	* The new ess_init() routine should recognize following chips:

		ESS 488/688/1788/1868/1869/1878/1887/1888

	* Support 16 bits audio playback/record

	* Audio capabilties showed by mpg123:

		|  s16  |  u16  |  u8   |  s8   | ulaw  | alaw  |
	    --------------------------------------------------------
	  8000  |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |
	 11025  |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |
	 12000  |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |
	 16000  |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |
	 22050  |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |
	 24000  |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |
	 32000  |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |
	 44100  |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |
	 48000  |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |  M/S  |       |

	* Due to lack of spec, this patch was ported from Luigi's new FreeBSD
	  sound driver & Linux's sound driver.

	* This patch has been tested on my laptop(ESS1869 built-in) for a
	  couple of weeks.

Fix: Which were not included in this patch:

		* MIDI playback.
		* Full duplex mode.
		* ESS 1887 specific record setting.

	* The ess_XXXX related routine should be kept into a separated file;
	  however, it seems that some other SB-clone drivers(JAZZ 16, SoundMan
	  Wave) shared the common routine(sb_XXXX) in sb_dsp.c & sb_mixer.c.
	  Any volunteer wants to make them more clear?
	
How-To-Repeat: 
	Please check & commit this patch if necessary, thanks.
